content:
# Joshua Chapter 22 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio pages 246-247
**Source Image:** `JOS_022_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_022_page_002.jpg`

---

**22:1** Then Joshua called to the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the half-tribe of Manasseh,

**22:2** and he said to them, "You have kept all that Moses, the servant of YHWH, commanded you, and you have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you.

**22:3** You have not forsaken your brothers these many days, to this day, and you have kept the charge of the commandment of YHWH your God.

**22:4** And now, YHWH your God has given rest to your brothers, as he spoke to them. And now, turn and go to your tents, to the land of your possession, which Moses, the servant of YHWH, gave to you on the other side of the Jordan.

**22:5** Only be very careful to do the commandment and the Torah that Moses, the servant of YHWH, commanded you: to love YHWH your God, and to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, and to cling to him, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul."

**22:6** And Joshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.

**22:7** And to the one half of the tribe of Manasseh, Moses had given a possession in the Bashan, and to its other half, Joshua gave a possession among their brothers on the other side of the Jordan, westward. And also when Joshua sent them away to their tents, he blessed them,

**22:8** and he spoke to them, saying, "With great wealth return to your tents, and with very much livestock, with silver, and with gold, and with bronze, and with iron, and with very many garments. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers."

**22:9** And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh returned and departed from the Sons of Israel, from Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan, to go to the land of Gilead, to the land of their possession, in which they had been given possession according to the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

**22:10** And they came to the region of the Jordan which is in the land of Canaan, and the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by the Jordan — an altar great in appearance.

**22:11** And the Sons of Israel heard it said, "Behold, the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built the altar opposite the land of Canaan, in the region of the Jordan, on the side belonging to the Sons of Israel."

**22:12** And when the Sons of Israel heard this, all the congregation of the Sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh to go up against them for war.

**22:13** And the Sons of Israel sent to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Manasseh, to the land of Gilead, Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest,

**22:14** and ten leaders with him — one leader for each father's house, for each of the tribes of Israel; and each one of them was the head of a father's house among the thousands of Israel.

**22:15** And they came to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Manasseh, to the land of Gilead, and they spoke with them, saying,

**22:16** "Thus says all the congregation of YHWH: 'What is this treachery that you have committed against the God of Israel, to turn away this day from after YHWH, by building for yourselves an altar, to rebel this day against YHWH?

**22:17** Is the iniquity of Peor too small for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, and there was a plague upon the congregation of YHWH,

**22:18** that you turn away this day from after YHWH? And it shall be, if you rebel today against YHWH, then tomorrow he will be angry with all the congregation of Israel.

**22:19** And indeed, if the land of your possession is unclean, then cross over to the land of the possession of YHWH, where the tabernacle of YHWH dwells, and take possession among us. But do not rebel against YHWH, and do not rebel against us, by building for yourselves an altar besides the altar of YHWH our God.

**22:20** Did not Achan the son of Zerah commit a treachery in the devoted thing, and wrath fell upon all the congregation of Israel? And that man did not perish alone in his iniquity.'"

**22:21** And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh answered, and they spoke to the heads of the thousands of Israel:

**22:22** "El, God, YHWH! El, God, YHWH! He knows! And Israel, it shall know! If in rebellion and if in treachery against YHWH — do not save us this day —

**22:23** if we have built for ourselves an altar to turn away from after YHWH, or if to offer upon it burnt offering or grain offering, or if to make upon it peace offerings — let YHWH himself seek it out.

**22:24** And if not, out of concern for this matter we have done this, saying, 'In time to come your sons may say to our sons, "What have you to do with YHWH, the God of Israel?

**22:25** For YHWH has set the Jordan as a boundary between us and you, sons of Reuben and sons of Gad. You have no portion in YHWH." And your sons will cause our sons to cease fearing YHWH.'

**22:26** And we said, 'Let us now act to build the altar — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice —

**22:27** but it shall be a witness between us and you, and between our generations after us, to perform the service of YHWH before him with our burnt offerings, and with our sacrifices, and with our peace offerings, so that your sons may not say to our sons in time to come, "You have no portion in YHWH."'

**22:28** And we said, 'And it shall be, when they say this to us or to our generations in time to come, then we shall say, "See the replica of the altar of YHWH which our fathers made — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice — but it is a witness between us and you."'

**22:29** Far be it from us to rebel against YHWH and to turn away this day from after YHWH, to build an altar for burnt offering, for grain offering, or for sacrifice, besides the altar of YHWH our God which is before his tabernacle."

**22:30** And when Phinehas the priest and the leaders of the congregation — the heads of the thousands of Israel who were with him — heard the words that the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the sons of Manasseh spoke, it was good in their eyes.

**22:31** And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest said to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Manasseh, "This day we know that YHWH is in our midst, because you have not committed this treachery against YHWH. Now you have delivered the Sons of Israel from the hand of YHWH."

**22:32** And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest and the leaders returned from the sons of Reuben and from the sons of Gad, from the land of Gilead, to the land of Canaan, to the Sons of Israel, and they brought back word to them.

**22:33** And the word was good in the eyes of the Sons of Israel, and the Sons of Israel blessed God. And they did not speak of going up against them for war, to destroy the land in which the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad were dwelling.

**22:34** And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad called the altar "Witness," for "it is a witness between us that YHWH is God."

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

### Method
Each verse was compared across all three translations (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) and checked against the Leningrad Codex manuscript images (pages 246-247). Where all three translators agreed on a reading, that agreement was adopted. Where they diverged, the Hebrew text was consulted and the wisest synthesis was chosen, favoring fidelity to the Hebrew while maintaining readable English. No Vaticanus images were available for this chapter.

### Agreements
The three translators demonstrated strong agreement across Joshua 22. All three preserve:
- **YHWH** (not "the LORD") throughout all thirty-four verses
- **Sons of Israel** (not "Israelites") for the national designation bene Yisra'el
- **"Treachery"** (ma'al) consistently for the covenantal unfaithfulness terminology
- **"Tabernacle of YHWH"** for mishkan YHWH (vv. 19, 29)
- The **threefold divine name** formula in v. 22 (El / Elohim / YHWH, repeated twice)
- **"Peor"** as the reference to the incident in Numbers 25
- The **altar as witness, not for sacrifice** distinction as the theological resolution
- **"Replica"** or **"pattern"** (tavnit) for the altar's function in v. 28
- The **Achan/Zerah** reference in v. 20 from the Leningrad Codex reading

### Major Decisions

**1. Proper name rendering: "Joshua" / "Jordan" / "Canaan" / "Gilead" (throughout)**
EMET uses Hebrew transliterations (Yehoshua, Yarden, Kenaan, Gilad) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use anglicized forms. The SOPHIA consensus adopts anglicized forms per established project convention, as these proper nouns have universally established English equivalents. See Disagreement Log.

**2. "Called to" vs. "summoned" vs. "called" (v. 1)**
EMET: "called to"; LOGOS/PNEUMA: "summoned." The Hebrew is va-yiqra, "and he called." SOPHIA adopts "called to" following EMET's more literal rendering of qara + el, which preserves the Hebrew preposition. "Summoned" is interpretive. See Disagreement Log.

**3. "Listened to my voice" vs. "obeyed my voice" (v. 2)**
EMET: "listened to my voice"; LOGOS/PNEUMA: "obeyed my voice." The Hebrew is shama'tem be-qoli. While shama' literally means "hear/listen," in this construction with be-qol it carries the performative sense of obedience. SOPHIA adopts "obeyed my voice" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) since the context is clearly about compliance, and the English "obeyed my voice" preserves the "voice" element while conveying the Hebrew idiom accurately. See Disagreement Log.

**4. "Do" vs. "observe" vs. "carry out" the commandment (v. 5)**
EMET: "to do" (la'asot); LOGOS: "to observe"; PNEUMA: "to carry out." The Hebrew la'asot means "to do/perform." SOPHIA adopts "to do" as the most literal rendering. See Disagreement Log.

**5. "With all your soul" vs. "with all your being" (v. 5)**
EMET/LOGOS: "with all your soul"; PNEUMA: "with all your being." The Hebrew nephesh is rendered "soul" by two translators. While PNEUMA correctly notes that nephesh is broader than the Greek psyche, "soul" is the traditional rendering and preserves the familiar Deuteronomic formula. SOPHIA adopts "soul" (EMET/LOGOS). See Disagreement Log.

**6. Verse 7 structure and "the Bashan" vs. "Bashan"**
EMET: "in the Bashan"; LOGOS/PNEUMA: "in Bashan." The Hebrew has the article: ba-Bashan. SOPHIA preserves "in the Bashan" (EMET) since the Hebrew article is present. For the second half, EMET has "its [other] half" while LOGOS/PNEUMA vary. SOPHIA synthesizes: "to its other half, Joshua gave a possession among their brothers." See Disagreement Log.

**7. "Regions" vs. "region" of the Jordan (vv. 10-11)**
EMET uses plural "regions" (gelilot); LOGOS/PNEUMA use singular "region." The Hebrew gelilot is indeed a plural form. SOPHIA adopts "region" (singular) since the plural in Hebrew functions as a geographic designation for the Jordan district as a single area, and the singular reads more naturally in English. See Disagreement Log.

**8. "An altar great in appearance" vs. "a large, conspicuous altar" (v. 10)**
EMET/LOGOS: "an altar great in appearance" (mizbeach gadol le-mar'eh); PNEUMA: "a large, conspicuous altar." SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew word order and idiom "an altar great in appearance" (EMET/LOGOS), which conveys both the size and the visual impact. See Disagreement Log.

**9. "Toward the face of" vs. "opposite" vs. "at the frontier of" (v. 11)**
EMET: "toward the face of the land of Canaan"; LOGOS: "opposite the land of Canaan"; PNEUMA: "at the frontier of the land of Canaan." The Hebrew el mul means "opposite/in front of." SOPHIA adopts "opposite" (LOGOS), which captures the spatial sense without over-literalizing. See Disagreement Log.

**10. "El, God, YHWH" vs. "The God of gods, YHWH" vs. "The Mighty One! God! YHWH!" (v. 22)**
EMET: "El, God, YHWH! El, God, YHWH!"; LOGOS: "The God of gods, YHWH! The God of gods, YHWH!"; PNEUMA: "The Mighty One! God! YHWH! The Mighty One! God! YHWH!" The Hebrew is El Elohim YHWH, El Elohim YHWH — three distinct divine names placed in apposition without conjunctions. LOGOS interprets El Elohim as a superlative ("God of gods"), which is grammatically possible but imports an interpretation. PNEUMA translates El as "The Mighty One," also interpretive. SOPHIA adopts EMET's literal rendering "El, God, YHWH" since the three names are juxtaposed in the Hebrew without grammatical subordination, and the reader should encounter the raw force of the oath formula. See Disagreement Log.

**11. "He shall know" vs. "it shall know" (v. 22)**
EMET: "he shall know"; LOGOS: "it shall know"; PNEUMA: "Israel itself will know." The Hebrew ve-Yisra'el hu yeda' — the pronoun hu could refer to Israel as a collective ("it") or as a personified entity ("he"). SOPHIA adopts "it shall know" (LOGOS), since Israel as a national entity takes the neuter in English. See Disagreement Log.

**12. "YHWH himself shall seek it out" vs. "let YHWH himself require it" (v. 23)**
EMET: "YHWH himself shall seek [it out]"; LOGOS: "let YHWH himself require it"; PNEUMA: "let YHWH himself call us to account." The Hebrew YHWH hu yevaqesh — the verb baqash means "to seek/require." SOPHIA adopts "let YHWH himself seek it out," combining the jussive sense from LOGOS/PNEUMA ("let") with EMET's more literal "seek" for baqash, adding "it out" for completeness. See Disagreement Log.

**13. "From fear of this matter" vs. "out of concern for a matter" vs. "out of anxiety" (v. 24)**
EMET: "from fear of [this] matter"; LOGOS: "out of concern for a matter"; PNEUMA: "out of anxiety — out of a specific concern." The Hebrew is mi-d'agah mi-davar. SOPHIA adopts "out of concern for this matter" (closest to LOGOS), which preserves both the causal preposition and the specific trigger (davar, "matter/thing"). See Disagreement Log.

**14. "Tomorrow" vs. "in time to come" vs. "someday" (vv. 24, 27, 28)**
EMET: "tomorrow"; LOGOS: "in time to come"; PNEUMA: "someday/in the future." The Hebrew machar here means not literally "tomorrow" but "in time to come, in the future." SOPHIA adopts "in time to come" (LOGOS), which conveys the Hebrew temporal sense without the misleading literalism of "tomorrow." See Disagreement Log.

**15. "Pattern" vs. "replica" (v. 28)**
EMET: "the pattern of the altar"; LOGOS/PNEUMA: "the replica of the altar." The Hebrew tavnit means "pattern, model, form, replica." Both readings are defensible. SOPHIA adopts "replica" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) since in this context the altar is explicitly described as a copy of the real altar — it is a reproduction, not an abstract blueprint. The word tavnit when used of the tabernacle in Exodus 25:9 means "pattern/blueprint," but here the referent is a physical structure modeled after another, making "replica" more precise. See Disagreement Log.

**16. "The altar [Ed]" vs. "the altar 'Witness'" vs. unnamed (v. 34)**
EMET: "called the altar [Ed]"; LOGOS: "called the altar —" (unnamed, with explanatory clause); PNEUMA: "named the altar 'Witness'." The LC text is syntactically compressed: va-yiqre'u ... la-mizbeach ki ed hu benotenu ki YHWH ha-Elohim. The name "Ed" (Witness) is implied rather than explicitly stated as a standalone name in the MT. SOPHIA adopts the explicit English name "Witness" in quotation marks (following PNEUMA), since the ki clause functions as the naming explanation and making the name explicit in English serves the reader. The Hebrew word ed is then preserved in the explanatory clause. See Disagreement Log.

**17. "Dwells" vs. "stands" for the tabernacle (v. 19)**
EMET: "where the tabernacle of YHWH dwells" (shakan); LOGOS: "where the tabernacle of YHWH stands"; PNEUMA: "where YHWH's tabernacle stands." The Hebrew shakan mishkan YHWH contains a deliberate wordplay: the root sh-k-n (to dwell) with its cognate noun mishkan (dwelling-place). SOPHIA adopts "dwells" (EMET) to preserve the Hebrew paronomasia — the tabernacle "dwells" because that is what a mishkan does. See Disagreement Log.

**18. "Delivered" vs. "rescued" (v. 31)**
EMET/LOGOS: "delivered"; PNEUMA: "rescued." The Hebrew hitzaltem is the Hiphil of natsal, "to deliver, rescue, snatch away." Both are valid. SOPHIA adopts "delivered" (EMET/LOGOS) as the more traditional and formal register for this priestly pronouncement. See Disagreement Log.

**19. "The word was good in the eyes" vs. "the matter was good in the eyes" vs. "the report pleased" (v. 33)**
EMET: "the word was good in the eyes"; LOGOS: "the matter was good in the eyes"; PNEUMA: "the report pleased." The Hebrew ha-davar va-yitav be-einei — davar can mean "word, matter, report, thing." SOPHIA adopts "the word was good in the eyes" (EMET), preserving both the Hebrew idiom be-einei ("in the eyes") and davar as "word" since the context is about the verbal report brought back. See Disagreement Log.

### Manuscript Observations (Leningrad Codex)
The Leningrad Codex pages 246-247 contain the full text of Joshua 22 in the standard three-column layout with Masoretic vocalization and cantillation marks. Notable observations:
- The text is well-preserved with clear lettering throughout both pages.
- In v. 20, the codex reads Akhan (not Akar as in Chronicles), consistent with all three translators' renderings.
- In v. 22, the threefold divine name formula El Elohim YHWH appears twice, with the Tetragrammaton clearly visible in both instances.
- In v. 34, the naming syntax is compressed as noted above; the name "Ed" is not set apart as a distinct word but flows into the ki-clause.
- No Vaticanus (LXX) images were available for comparison on this chapter.

---
